dublinCore.dc:title: 119 S3157 IS: School Meals for Healthy Kids Act of 2025
dublinCore.dc:rights: Pursuant to Title 17 Section 105 of the United States Code, this file is not subject to copyright protection and is in the public domain.

form.current-chamber: IN THE SENATE OF THE UNITED STATES

action.action-desc: Mr. Luján (for himself and Mr. Marshall) introduced the following bill; which was read twice and referred to the Committee on Agriculture, Nutrition, and Forestry

form.official-title: To amend the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act to improve direct certification, and for other purposes.

section.text: This Act may be cited as the School Meals for Healthy Kids Act of 2025.

section.header: Codification of existing use of data for direct certification
section.text: Section 9(b)(15) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1758(b)(15)) is amended by adding at the end the following:

subparagraph.text: Any State agency that uses Medicaid program data to directly certify children for free or reduced price lunches under this Act and free or reduced price breakfasts under section 4 of the Child Nutrition Act of 1966 (42 U.S.C. 1773) for the school year beginning on July 1, 2024, through the demonstration projects carried out pursuant to this Act may continue using Medicaid program data for subsequent school years in accordance with the conditions in effect at the start of the school year beginning on July 1, 2024.

section.header: Codification of existing methodology for community eligibility provision schools
section.text: Section 11(a)(1)(F) of the Richard B. Russell National School Lunch Act (42 U.S.C. 1759a(a)(1)(F)) is amended—

paragraph.text: in clause (i), by striking or successor regulations and inserting as in effect on the date of enactment of the School Meals for Healthy Kids Act of 2025;
by striking clause (vii) and inserting the following:

clause.text: For each school year, the multiplier shall be 1.6.
